note: Sourced disease-specific Patient-Zero Stage II overrides. Values are case-sampling priors, not disease-selection prevalence. Age/sex weights are calibrated from official prevalence tables where available; severity_level maps NIMH/CDC impairment categories to mild, moderate, and severe. When pediatric and adult measures use different recall windows, weights are harmonized heuristically and documented here rather than treated as exact epidemiologic estimates.

methodology.severity_level: NIMH reports 'serious impairment'; this is mapped to 'severe'. Where only moderate/serious are reported, mild is kept as a small generation prior rather than forced to zero.
methodology.age_strata: Child weights use adolescent/child prevalence when available; Adult and Elderly use adult age bands. Because source age bands do not exactly match 6-17, 18-64, and 65-90, values are normalized approximations.
methodology.biological_sex: Sex weights are normalized from male/female prevalence rates and the global sex prior.

source.title: NIMH Major Depression Statistics
source.evidence: Adult and adolescent prevalence by sex/age; adolescent severe impairment; SDS severe-impairment caveats.

age_strata.method: labels are normalized Patient-Zero sampling weights mapped from source age bands to Child/Adult/Elderly.
